prevalence.trend: increasing with aging and improved survival

epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: Antibody testing and electrophysiology widely available; thymoma evaluation standard

publicHealthSystem.controlRate: Moderate to high with access to immunotherapy and crisis management; varies by member state

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: High access to antibody testing, EMG, and specialist neuromuscular clinics in many countries
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Pyridostigmine, immunosuppression, IVIG/plasmapheresis for crisis, thymectomy when indicated; biologics in refractory cases in some systems
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Access variability and management of long-term immunosuppression risks
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: Robust specialist care and access to crisis therapies in many member states
